message:
fix: explicit error types, sleep handle cleanup, and debug_assert removal

Four targeted source quality improvements:

Sleep thread handle cleanup (src/time/sleep.rs):
- Replace `let _ = handle.join()` with `drop(handle)` in reset(), cancel(),
  Future::poll(), and Drop::drop() — the intent is to detach fallback timer
  threads, not block on their completion. join() would block the executor;
  drop() correctly detaches the JoinHandle while the thread continues running.
- Add ubs:ignore comment on the intentional detach pattern

Explicit error type annotations (src/trace/file.rs, src/types/typed_symbol.rs):
- Add explicit type annotations to lz4_flex::DecompressError, bincode::EncodeError,
  and bincode::DecodeError in .map_err() closures
- Prevents ambiguous type inference if upstream crate error types change and
  makes the error conversion chain self-documenting

Remove redundant debug_asserts (src/util/resource.rs):
- Remove debug_assert! checks in SymbolPool::deallocate() that duplicate the
  runtime guard immediately below them — the if-return already handles both
  the !in_use and wrong-length cases, making the debug_asserts pure noise

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
